purpose: Classify RedClaw workspace skills by trust level so experimental skills do not silently control important behavior.

core.meaning: Allowed to shape default behavior when explicitly enabled in agent config.

verified.meaning: Usable after passing validation and a real task test.
requirements: does not conflict with core skills

experimental.meaning: A shot-in-the-dark skill. May be inspected, but should not be relied on for critical behavior.
requirements: must not be auto-enabled for sensitive agents
must not send/delete/mutate external state without explicit approval
must not override Maton, Mempalace, or RedClaw safety rules

quarantined.meaning: Do not use until reviewed or repaired.
requirements: requires explicit user instruction to inspect

safetyRules: Unknown skills are experimental until audited.
Experimental skills must not be included in default agent allowlists.
Skills that touch email, money, credentials, public posts, infrastructure, or production require explicit trust classification.
Maton remains the email authority even if another email-related skill exists.
Mempalace remains the durable-memory authority even if another memory-related skill exists.
